description: Send email via SMTP (OCI Email Delivery or any relay) and read IMAP mailboxes.

tools.description:
Sends an outbound email message through a configured SMTP relay (OCI Email Delivery, Gmail, Office 365, or any standards-compliant SMTP server).

When to use: Use when the user asks to send, email, notify, alert, message, or forward something to one or more recipients, or when the agent needs to tell a human a flow is awaiting input. Supports plain text and HTML bodies, CC/BCC, and priority hints.

How to discover inputs: Recipient addresses, subject, and body come from the user or from prior tool output — ask the user if any are missing. The sender (`from_addr`) and SMTP credentials are pre-configured in tool conf; do not ask the user for them.

How to communicate: After sending, confirm in one sentence with the recipient and subject. Do not echo the full body back.

Example: user says 'email the report to alice@acme.com'. Call {to:'alice@acme.com', subject:'Weekly Report', body:'See attached summary...', content_type:'plain'}.

schema.name: to
schema.description: Primary recipient address or addresses. Single email or a comma/semicolon-separated list like `alice@acme.com,bob@acme.com`. Required.

schema.name: subject
schema.description: Subject line shown in the recipient's inbox. Keep under ~80 characters for best readability. Required.

schema.name: body
schema.description:
Message body content. Plain text by default; set `content_type='html'` (or `html=true`) to send HTML markup like `

Hello

`. Required.
examples:
Hi Alice,

The weekly report is attached. Let me know if you have questions.

-- Bot
Pipeline run #4821 failed at step 'extract'. Logs: https://example.com/run/4821

schema.name: cc
schema.description: Carbon-copy recipient address(es). Comma/semicolon-separated list, same format as `to`. Optional; omit if not needed.

schema.name: bcc
schema.description: Blind carbon-copy recipient address(es). Comma/semicolon-separated list, same format as `to`. Optional; recipients in this list are hidden from other recipients.

schema.name: html
schema.description: Set `true` if `body` contains HTML markup, `false` (default) for plain text. Equivalent to `content_type='html'`. Optional; defaults to `false`.

schema.name: content_type
schema.description: Body MIME content type. Use `plain` for unformatted text, `html` for formatted markup. Optional; defaults to `plain`. Overrides the `html` boolean when both are set.

schema.name: priority
schema.description: Message priority hint that sets the `X-Priority` header. Use `high` for urgent alerts, `low` for FYI notices, `normal` (default) for routine mail. Optional.

_confDescriptions.smtp_host: SMTP server hostname (e.g. smtp.email.us-ashburn-1.oci.oraclecloud.com for OCI Email Delivery).
_confDescriptions.smtp_port: SMTP server port. 587 for STARTTLS (default), 465 for SSL, 25 for plain.
_confDescriptions.smtp_username: (secret) SMTP auth username. OCI Console → Identity → Users → SMTP Credentials. Provide via secret store.
_confDescriptions.smtp_password: (secret) SMTP auth password — DO NOT commit. Provide via secret store at deploy time.
_confDescriptions.from_addr: Sender 'From' address. Must be a verified/approved sender on your SMTP relay.
_confDescriptions.use_starttls: If true, upgrade plaintext connection to TLS via STARTTLS (typical for port 587).
_confDescriptions.use_ssl: If true, use implicit SSL/TLS from the start (typical for port 465). Mutually exclusive with STARTTLS.
_confDescriptions.max_retries: Number of retry attempts on transient SMTP failures.
_confDescriptions.allowed_recipient_domains: Optional comma-separated allowlist of recipient domains (e.g. 'acme.com,partner.io'). Empty = no restriction.

tools.description:
Fetches messages from an IMAP mailbox (Gmail, Office 365, or any standards-compliant IMAP server) and returns a structured list with headers and body.

When to use: Use when the user asks to read, check, list, search, summarize, or pull email from an inbox or folder — for example 'show me unread mail', 'any emails from Alice this week?', or 'summarize today's inbox'. Default to listing first when the target message is unknown.

How to discover inputs: Folder defaults to `INBOX` — only set `folder` if the user names a specific mailbox. If the user hasn't named one, list `INBOX` first, then summarize subjects/senders and ask which message they care about. IMAP server and credentials are pre-configured in tool conf.

How to communicate: Summarize results in plain prose (sender, subject, date, one-line snippet). Never dump raw `body_html` or full headers unless explicitly asked.

Example: user says 'any unread mail today?'. Call {folder:'INBOX', unread_only:true, since:'2026-06-16', limit:25, fields:'uid,from,subject,date,body_text'}.

schema.name: folder
schema.description: IMAP mailbox folder name to read. Defaults to `INBOX`. Use vendor-specific paths like `[Gmail]/All Mail` or `Sent` for non-default folders. Optional.

schema.name: limit
schema.description: Maximum number of messages to return in one call. Capped server-side by the `max_messages` conf value. Optional; defaults to 25.

schema.name: unread_only
schema.description: If `true`, only return messages with the UNSEEN flag (not yet opened). Optional; defaults to `false` which returns all messages matching other filters.

schema.name: since
schema.description: Lower-bound date filter; only fetches messages on/after this date. Accepts ISO-8601 (`YYYY-MM-DD`) or IMAP-native date (`DD-Mon-YYYY`). Optional; omit for no date filter.

schema.name: fields
schema.description: Comma-separated list of per-message fields to include in the response. Allowed: `uid`, `from`, `to`, `subject`, `date`, `body_text`, `body_html`, `headers`. Optional; defaults to `uid,from,to,subject,date,body_text`. Omit `body_html` and `headers` unless explicitly needed.

schema.name: mark_seen
schema.description: If `true`, set the Seen/Read flag on each fetched message. Optional; defaults to `false` so calls act as a non-destructive peek.

_confDescriptions.imap_port: IMAP server port. 993 for SSL (default), 143 for plain/STARTTLS.
_confDescriptions.imap_username: (secret) IMAP login (usually the full email address). Provide via secret store.
_confDescriptions.imap_password: (secret) IMAP password or app-password — DO NOT commit. Provide via secret store at deploy time.
_confDescriptions.use_ssl: If true, use implicit SSL/TLS (typical for port 993).
_confDescriptions.max_messages: Hard cap on messages returned per call, regardless of requested limit.
_confDescriptions.max_body_bytes: Per-message body byte cap; longer bodies are truncated with truncated:true.
